title: Study synopsis
protocol_text: KLIN-ONC-DEMO-001 is a synthetic Phase II, open-label oncology study of KLIN-201 in adult subjects with advanced or metastatic solid tumors and measurable disease by RECIST 1.1. Safety labs, demographics, baseline tumor identification, longitudinal tumor measurements, and response assessments are collected throughout treatment.
data_needed: Demographics, pathology confirmation, radiology reports, lab reports, investigator response assessments

title: Key eligibility
protocol_text: Subjects must be at least 18 years old, have signed informed consent before screening assessments, have histologically or cytologically confirmed advanced/metastatic malignancy, ECOG performance status 0-2, and at least one measurable lesion at baseline by RECIST 1.1.
data_needed: Age, consent date, pathology confirmation, ECOG, baseline lesions

title: Tumor assessment schedule
protocol_text: Baseline tumor imaging must be performed before treatment start. Follow-up tumor assessments are scheduled every 8 weeks through Week 48. The allowed assessment window is plus or minus 7 days from the nominal visit date unless clinically justified.
data_needed: Assessment dates, visit labels, treatment start date, radiology source

title: Target and non-target lesion rules
protocol_text: Target lesions must be uniquely identified at baseline and carried forward across all post-baseline assessments using the same lesion link ID. A tumor result record at a follow-up visit must refer to a known baseline lesion or to a documented new lesion. Non-target disease status must be tracked consistently across response assessments.
data_needed: TULNKID, TRLNKID, lesion location, tumor state, source radiology

title: RECIST response rules
protocol_text: Partial Response requires at least a 30% decrease in target lesion sum from baseline, no new lesions, and no unequivocal non-target progression. Complete Response requires disappearance of all target lesions, no new lesions, and resolution/absence of all non-target lesions. Any new lesion is treated as progression unless adjudicated otherwise.
data_needed: Target lesion sums, non-target response, new lesion response, overall response

title: Safety laboratory assessments
protocol_text: Hematology and chemistry panels are collected at baseline and each response visit. Units must be standardized for analysis. Clinical-analysis checks are performed during SDTM-to-ADaM lab derivation to identify physiologically impossible combinations, including direct bilirubin greater than total bilirubin.
data_needed: LBTESTCD, LBSTRESN, LBSTRESU, LBDTC, LBNRIND, paired bilirubin analytes

title: Data review and query handling
protocol_text: Critical protocol or medical logic conflicts must block save or require adjudication before analysis output generation. Warnings should raise a query. Suggested changes should be presented as non-blocking standardization improvements.
data_needed: Severity, check layer, message, suggested action, reviewer decision
